description:
With Stream Deck, unleash your creative genius and make waves across Twitch and YouTube. All while focusing on what matters most your audience. As a broadcaster that likes to produce on the fly and have high quality production.


What's new in Stream Deck 7.3?
Introducing Stream Deck + XL
Our biggest Stream Deck yet. Stream Deck + XL brings 36 LCD keys and 6 dials to the full Stream Deck + experience — more room to build, more controls at your fingertips, and all the tactile, real-time precision Stream Deck + is known for.
Discover Stream Deck + XL

🎙️ Introducing Wave Next — The future of creator audio starts here
Wave Link 3.0 is the software backbone of Wave Next — built from the ground up to unlock everything the Wave FX Processor makes possible. Whether you're setting up the Wave:3 MK.2, Wave XLR MK.2, XLR Dock MK.2, or the all-new Wave XLR Pro, Wave Link 3.0 gives you full control over your hardware's Clipguard 2.0 protection, onboard DSP effects, , and VST Insert capabilities — all from one place.
Pair that with a Stream Deck + XL and you've got hands-on control over your entire audio setup without ever leaving your workflow.
Discover Wave Next
Watch the Wave Next announcement

Meet the all new Wave Link 3.0 plugin
Shape your sound from Stream Deck with the Wave Link plugin. Built for Wave Link 3.0, this plugin puts full control of your audio mix at your fingertips — adjust volume, mute inputs, trigger effects, and switch between mixes in real time using keys or dials. This release brings overhauled visuals, a new press-and-hold mode for effects — perfect for momentary triggers like a voice changer — plus fading with an option for smooth level transitions. Add to Stream Deck lets you generate a ready-made Wave Link profile for your device in one click, and under-the-hood improvements deliver faster level meters, a more reliable connection process, and numerous bug fixes.
👉 Get the Wave Link 3.0 plugin
Requires Wave Link 3.0 and is not backwards compatible with Wave Link 2.0

​ Performance improvements & bug fixes
- Fixed an issue where installing plugins via in-app search or the missing plugins dialog failed on the first attempt. Plugins from Marketplace will now install correctly straight away.
- Fixed an issue where Galleon 100 SD sporadically returned to hardware mode. Your Galleon 100 SD will now stay reliably connected to the Stream Deck app.
- Improved Smart Profile detection for games running in Borderless Windowed mode. Profiles will now switch correctly when alt-tabbing in and out of borderless windowed games.
- Fixed an issue where auto-installed profiles from plugins couldn't be switched to another profile from within the plugin.
- Fixed an issue where copying a page multiple times failed to carry over nested folders.
- Fixed an issue where the Auto Software Detection back-to-profile action could leave stale icons on screen instead of returning to the previous profile.
- Fixed a crash that could occur when triggering navigation actions inside a Key Logic action.
- Fixed a crash when a plugin monitoring applications was reloaded via deep-link.
- Fixed a number of crashes and issues related to app shutdown, plugin shutdown, and system log-off. (Windows)
- Fixed a crash that could occur with the Website action due to a file path conversion issue. (Windows)

Want to share feedback on Stream Deck 7.3? Connect with us:
- Leave a comment on our Subreddit
- Chat with fellow community members on Discord
- Hit up @elgato on X
- Discover more on Explorer
- Contact our Support Team
